title: vicia cracca subsp. oreophila
intro: Vicia cracca subsp. oreophila, also known as the alpine or alpine hairy vetch, is a plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Vicia cracca subsp. oreophila include reducing inflammation, calming the digestive system, and acting as a natural diuretic. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and in some cases, it may also be used as a dried extract or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
